speaker: KING
text: Thus ever did rebellion find rebuke.—

speaker: KING
text: Ill-spirited Worcester, did not we send grace,

speaker: KING
text: Pardon, and terms of love to all of you?

speaker: KING
text: And wouldst thou turn our offers contrary,

speaker: KING
text: Misuse the tenor of thy kinsman’s trust?

speaker: KING
text: Three knights upon our party slain today,

speaker: KING
text: A noble earl, and many a creature else

speaker: KING
text: Had been alive this hour

speaker: KING
text: If, like a Christian, thou hadst truly borne

speaker: KING
text: Betwixt our armies true intelligence.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: What I have done my safety urged me to.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And I embrace this fortune patiently,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Since not to be avoided it falls on me.

speaker: KING
text: Bear Worcester to the death, and Vernon too.

speaker: KING
text: Other offenders we will pause upon.

speaker: KING
text: How goes the field?

speaker: PRINCE
text: The noble Scot, Lord Douglas, when he saw

speaker: PRINCE
text: The fortune of the day quite turned from him,

speaker: PRINCE
text: The noble Percy slain, and all his men

speaker: PRINCE
text: Upon the foot of fear, fled with the rest,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And, falling from a hill, he was so bruised

speaker: PRINCE
text: That the pursuers took him. At my tent

speaker: PRINCE
text: The Douglas is, and I beseech your Grace

speaker: PRINCE
text: I may dispose of him.

speaker: KING
text: With all my heart.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Then, brother John of Lancaster, to you

speaker: PRINCE
text: This honorable bounty shall belong.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Go to the Douglas and deliver him

speaker: PRINCE
text: Up to his pleasure, ransomless and free.

speaker: PRINCE
text: His valors shown upon our crests today

speaker: PRINCE
text: Have taught us how to cherish such high deeds,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Even in the bosom of our adversaries.

speaker: LANCASTER
text: I thank your Grace for this high courtesy,

speaker: LANCASTER
text: Which I shall give away immediately.

speaker: KING
text: Then this remains, that we divide our power.

speaker: KING
text: You, son John, and my cousin Westmoreland,

speaker: KING
text: Towards York shall bend you with your dearest speed

speaker: KING
text: To meet Northumberland and the prelate Scroop,

speaker: KING
text: Who, as we hear, are busily in arms.

speaker: KING
text: Myself and you, son Harry, will towards Wales

speaker: KING
text: To fight with Glendower and the Earl of March.

speaker: KING
text: Rebellion in this land shall lose his sway,

speaker: KING
text: Meeting the check of such another day.

speaker: KING
text: And since this business so fair is done,

speaker: KING
text: Let us not leave till all our own be won.
